title: Deligne Absolute Hodge — What Makes Abelian Varieties Special

analysis.object: T734: Deligne Absolute Hodge — What Makes Abelian Varieties Special depth analysis

deligne_theorem.description: Deligne 1982: absolute Hodge classes on abelian varieties are algebraic
deligne_theorem.reason: Proved -- the only non-trivial proved surjectivity case

absolute_hodge_defn.description: Absolute Hodge class: remains Hodge under all embeddings of C

abelian_variety_special.description: Abelian varieties: group structure = EML-2 (translation = linear)
abelian_variety_special.reason: Group law makes cycles trackable via EML-2 structure

cm_abelian_varieties.description: CM abelian varieties: even more rigid -- Galois acts via CM type

depth_condition_separating.description: Abelian varieties have extra EML-2 structure (group law) absent in general varieties
depth_condition_separating.reason: The group law is the depth condition that closes the gap

generalization_barrier.description: General varieties lack group structure -- no EML-2 translation
generalization_barrier.reason: Loss of EML-2 group structure -> gap reopens

t734_theorem.description: Deligne proved case = EML-2 group structure. Open case = no group. T734.
t734_theorem.reason: Group structure is the precise depth condition separating proved from open

analysis.theorem: T734: Deligne Absolute Hodge — What Makes Abelian Varieties Special (S1013).

key_theorem: T734: Deligne Absolute Hodge — What Makes Abelian Varieties Special (S1013).
